title: National anthem of Slovenia

infobox.value: Where the last word can be capitalized to mean "Slavic" instead of "Glory", but despite a popular interpretation that it could refer to the Slavic people in general, the word *slava* was written uncapitalised by Jenko. It was capitalised by public in 1863.[[5]](#cite_note-Kristen2007-5) Nowadays, it is written with small letters.[[6]](#cite_note-Lisjak2008-6)

The question whether the entire "Zdravljica" or only its seventh stanza constitutes the Slovene national anthem, remains unresolved. Whereas the [Constitution of Slovenia](/wiki/Constitution_of_Slovenia "Constitution of Slovenia") determines the title of the poem, the *Act about the anthem* specifically determines its seventh stanza. It has been argued that the act contradicts the constitution and that the question should be resolved by the [Slovenian Constitutional Court](/wiki/Constitutional_Court_of_Slovenia "Constitutional Court of Slovenia").[[8]](#cite_note-Cernic-9)[[9]](#cite_note-Delo2012-10) In practice, mostly only the seventh stanza is sung and reproduced as the national anthem.

Danica Veceric (2006). [*Slovenia. Looking at Europe*](https://books.google.com/books?id=JQV6kAwT4v0C&dq=Zdravljica++French+Revolution&pg=PA24), The Oliver Press, Inc., [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1881508749](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1881508749 "Special:BookSources/1881508749")

Rupnik, Janko; Cijan, Rafael; Grafenauer, Božo (1993). [*Ustavno pravo*](https://books.google.com/books?id=-INFAQAAIAAJ) [*Constitutional Law*] (in Slovenian). Faculty of Law, University of Maribor. p. 51. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-961-6009-39-3](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-961-6009-39-3 "Special:BookSources/978-961-6009-39-3").

Javornik, Marijan, ed. (2001). [*Enciklopedija Slovenije*](https://books.google.com/books?id=jDEOAQAAMAAJ) [*Encyclopedia of Slovenia*] (in Slovenian). Vol. 15. Mladinska knjiga. p. 403. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-86-11-14288-3](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-86-11-14288-3 "Special:BookSources/978-86-11-14288-3").

["Državni simboli in znamka Slovenije"](http://www.dvajset.si/prvih-20/pregled/prej-in-zdaj/drzavni-simboli/) [National Symbols and the Trademark of Slovenia] (in Slovenian). Government Communication Office of the Republic of Slovenia. 2011. Retrieved 3 March 2012.

Kristen, Samo (2007). ["V Pragi "Naprej zastava slave", v Ljubljani "Kde domov můj?" Jan Masaryk in Slovenci"](https://web.archive.org/web/20160304002017/http://www.anthropos.si/anthropos/2007/3_4/17_kristen.pdf) [In Prague "Naprej zastava slave", in Ljubljana "Kde domov můj?" Jan Masaryk and Slovenes] (PDF). *Anthropos* (in Slovenian). **39** (3–4): 272–274. [ISSN](/wiki/ISSN_(identifier) "ISSN (identifier)") [0587-5161](https://search.worldcat.org/issn/0587-5161). [COBISS](/wiki/COBISS_(identifier) "COBISS (identifier)") [11065421](https://plus.cobiss.net/cobiss/si/en/bib/11065421). Archived from [the original](http://www.anthropos.si/anthropos/2007/3_4/17_kristen.pdf) (PDF) on 2016-03-04. Retrieved 2013-10-24.

Lisjak Gabrijelčič, Luka (2008). [*The Dissolution of the Slavic Identity of the Slovenes in the 1980s. The case of the Venetic Theory*](https://web.archive.org/web/20171201044323/http://www.etd.ceu.hu/2008/lisjak-gabrijelcic_luka.pdf) (PDF). Department of History, Central European University. p. 34. Archived from [the original](http://www.etd.ceu.hu/2008/lisjak-gabrijelcic_luka.pdf) (PDF) on 2017-12-01. Retrieved 2013-10-24.

Državna založba Slovenije [State Publishing House of Slovenia]: 958–959. [ISSN](/wiki/ISSN_(identifier) "ISSN (identifier)") [1318-2242](https://search.worldcat.org/issn/1318-2242). first_paragraph: The [national anthem](/wiki/National_anthem "National anthem") of [Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia") is one of the verses of "[Zdravljica](/wiki/Zdravljica "Zdravljica")", a *[carmen figuratum](/wiki/Carmen_figuratum "Carmen figuratum")* [poem](/wiki/Poem "Poem") by the 19th-century [Romantic](/wiki/Romantic_poetry "Romantic poetry") [Slovene poet](/wiki/Slovene_poet "Slovene poet") [France Prešeren](/wiki/France_Pre%C5%A1eren "France Prešeren"), inspired by the ideals of *[Liberté, égalité, fraternité](/wiki/Libert%C3%A9,_%C3%A9galit%C3%A9,_fraternit%C3%A9 "Liberté, égalité, fraternité")*,[[1]](#cite_note-1) and set to music by [Stanko Premrl](/wiki/Stanko_Premrl "Stanko Premrl"). It is one of the [national symbols of Slovenia](/wiki/National_symbols_of_Slovenia "National symbols of Slovenia"), along with its [flag](/wiki/Flag_of_Slovenia "Flag of Slovenia") and [coat of arms](/wiki/Coat_of_arms_of_Slovenia "Coat of arms of Slovenia").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Zdravljica

| English: '[Toast](/wiki/Toast_(honor) "Toast (honor)")' | |
| --- | --- |
| The national anthem of Slovenia as defined in the 1994 law adopting it. As typically performed, it consists of a part of the "[Zdravljica](/wiki/Zdravljica "Zdravljica")" poem, specifically the 7th stanza, which is frequently regarded as the national anthem. | |
| ---  National anthem of Slovenia  --- | |
| Lyrics | [France Prešeren](/wiki/France_Pre%C5%A1eren "France Prešeren"), 1844 |
| Music | [Stanko Premrl](/wiki/Stanko_Premrl "Stanko Premrl"), 1905 |
| Adopted | 1989 (as regional anthem) 1994 (as national anthem) |
| Preceded by | "[Naprej, zastava slave](/wiki/Naprej,_zastava_slave "Naprej, zastava slave")" |
|  | |
| Audio sample | |
| [U.S. Navy Band](/wiki/U.S._Navy_Band "U.S. Navy Band") instrumental rendition in [B-flat major](/wiki/B-flat_major "B-flat major")  * [file](/wiki/File:Slovene_national_anthem,_performed_by_the_United_States_Navy_Band.oga "File:Slovene national anthem, performed by the United States Navy Band.oga") * [help](/wiki/Help:Media "Help:Media") | |
|  | |

The [national anthem](/wiki/National_anthem "National anthem") of [Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia") is one of the verses of "[Zdravljica](/wiki/Zdravljica "Zdravljica")", a *[carmen figuratum](/wiki/Carmen_figuratum "Carmen figuratum")* [poem](/wiki/Poem "Poem") by the 19th-century [Romantic](/wiki/Romantic_poetry "Romantic poetry") [Slovene poet](/wiki/Slovene_poet "Slovene poet") [France Prešeren](/wiki/France_Pre%C5%A1eren "France Prešeren"), inspired by the ideals of *[Liberté, égalité, fraternité](/wiki/Libert%C3%A9,_%C3%A9galit%C3%A9,_fraternit%C3%A9 "Liberté, égalité, fraternité")*,[[1]](#cite_note-1) and set to music by [Stanko Premrl](/wiki/Stanko_Premrl "Stanko Premrl"). It is one of the [national symbols of Slovenia](/wiki/National_symbols_of_Slovenia "National symbols of Slovenia"), along with its [flag](/wiki/Flag_of_Slovenia "Flag of Slovenia") and [coat of arms](/wiki/Coat_of_arms_of_Slovenia "Coat of arms of Slovenia").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_anthem_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_anthem_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Background")]

Historically, the national anthem from 1860[[2]](#cite_note-UP1993-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) until the beginning of the 1990s,[[4]](#cite_note-4) was "[Naprej, zastava slave](/wiki/Naprej,_zastava_slave "Naprej, zastava slave")" ("Forward, Flag of Glory"),[[i]](#cite_note-7) the first ever piece of [Slovene](/wiki/Slovene_language "Slovene language") literature to be translated into English.[[7]](#cite_note-Dobrovoljc1951-8)

### Lyrics and music

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_anthem_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Lyrics and music")]

The words of the current Slovene national anthem are all or part[[ii]](#cite_note-11) of "[Zdravljica](/wiki/Zdravljica "Zdravljica")", written by the 19th-century Slovene poet [France Prešeren](/wiki/France_Pre%C5%A1eren "France Prešeren") for which music was written by the Slovene composer [Stanko Premrl](/wiki/Stanko_Premrl "Stanko Premrl") in 1905. Emphasising [internationalism](/wiki/Internationalism_(politics) "Internationalism (politics)"),[[10]](#cite_note-Bozic-12) it was defined in 1994[[11]](#cite_note-13) as the anthem with the *Act on the national symbols of Slovenia*. However, even before the [breakup of Yugoslavia](/wiki/Breakup_of_Yugoslavia "Breakup of Yugoslavia"), the lyrics and music were together adopted as the regional anthem of the [Socialist Republic of Slovenia](/wiki/Socialist_Republic_of_Slovenia "Socialist Republic of Slovenia") on 27 September 1989. Therefore, it was the regional anthem of the [Socialist Republic of Slovenia](/wiki/Socialist_Republic_of_Slovenia "Socialist Republic of Slovenia") (known as simply the "Republic of Slovenia" from 1990 to 1991) as a constituent republic of [Yugoslavia](/wiki/Socialist_Federal_Republic_of_Yugoslavia "Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia") from 8 March 1990 to 25 June 1991, as well.

### Legal status

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_anthem_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Legal status")]

As a work of arts, published in the official journal *[Official Gazette](/w/index.php?title=Official_Gazette_(Slovenia)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Official Gazette (Slovenia) (page does not exist)")* (*Uradni list*), the text and melody of the seventh stanza of "Zdravljica" qualify as an official work and are per Article 9 of the Slovene Copyright and Related Rights Act not protected by the copyrights.[[12]](#cite_note-14) Their usage is regulated by the *Act Regulating the Coat-of-Arms, Flag and Anthem of the Republic of Slovenia and the Flag of the Slovene Nation* (*Zakon o grbu, zastavi in himni Republike Slovenije ter o slovenski narodni zastavi*), published in the *Official Gazette* in 1994.[[13]](#cite_note-15) The official melody is played in [B-flat major](/wiki/B-flat_major "B-flat major").

## Lyrics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_anthem_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Lyrics")]

| Slovene original[[14]](#cite_note-protokol-16) | English translation[[15]](#cite_note-J_Lavrin_1954-17) ([Janko Lavrin](/wiki/Janko_Lavrin "Janko Lavrin"), 1954) |
| --- | --- |
| |  |  | | --- | --- | | Žive naj vsi narodi  Ki hrepene dočakat' dan  Da koder sonce hodi  Prepir iz sveta bo pregnan    Da rojak  Prost bo vsak  Ne vrag, le sosed bo mejak!    Da rojak  Prost bo vsak  Ne vrag, le sosed bo  Ne vrag, le sosed bo mejak! | [ʒi.ʋe naj ʍsi nàː.ɾo.di]  [ki xɾɛ.pɛ.ne dɔ.t͡ʃàː.kad‿dáːn]  [dá kòː.dəɾ sóːn.t͡sɛ xo.di]  [pɾɛ.piɾ is sʋe.ta bóː pɾɛg.nàn ‖]    [dá ɾɔ.jàk]  [pɾɔ́zd‿bóː ʍsàk]  [nɛ́ ʋɾàk lɛ́ sɔ̀ː.sɛd‿bóː mɛ.jàːk ‖]    [dá ɾɔ.jàk]  [pɾɔ́zd‿bóː ʍsàk]  [nɛ́ ʋɾàk lɛ́ sɔ̀ː.sɛd‿bóː]  [nɛ́ ʋɾàg‿lɛ́ sɔ̀ː.sɛd‿bóː mɛ.jàːk ‖][[a]](#cite_note-18) | | Let all people of all nations live  Who long and work for that bright day  When o'er earth's habitations  No war, no strife shall hold its sway    Who long to see  That all men free  No more shall foes, but neighbours be!    Who long to see  That all men free  No more shall foes, but neighbours,  No more shall foes, but neighbours be! |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/29/%C5%BDive_naj_vsi_narodi_Pre%C5%A1eren_Schuman.jpg/250px-%C5%BDive_naj_vsi_narodi_Pre%C5%A1eren_Schuman.jpg)](/wiki/File:%C5%BDive_naj_vsi_narodi_Pre%C5%A1eren_Schuman.jpg)

A sculpture with the first line of the 7th stanza of the poem, translated into various languages, near the [Schuman roundabout](/wiki/Schuman_roundabout "Schuman roundabout"), [Brussels](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels")

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_anthem_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-7)** Where the last word can be capitalized to mean "Slavic" instead of "Glory", but despite a popular interpretation that it could refer to the Slavic people in general, the word *slava* was written uncapitalised by Jenko. It was capitalised by public in 1863.[[5]](#cite_note-Kristen2007-5) Nowadays, it is written with small letters.[[6]](#cite_note-Lisjak2008-6)
2. **[^](#cite_ref-11)** The question whether the entire "Zdravljica" or only its seventh stanza constitutes the Slovene national anthem, remains unresolved. Whereas the [Constitution of Slovenia](/wiki/Constitution_of_Slovenia "Constitution of Slovenia") determines the title of the poem, the *Act about the anthem* specifically determines its seventh stanza. It has been argued that the act contradicts the constitution and that the question should be resolved by the [Slovenian Constitutional Court](/wiki/Constitutional_Court_of_Slovenia "Constitutional Court of Slovenia").[[8]](#cite_note-Cernic-9)[[9]](#cite_note-Delo2012-10) In practice, mostly only the seventh stanza is sung and reproduced as the national anthem.

1. **[^](#cite_ref-18)** See [Help:IPA/Slovenian](/wiki/Help:IPA/Slovenian "Help:IPA/Slovenian") and [Slovenian phonology](/wiki/Slovenian_phonology "Slovenian phonology").
